transcript: This island next to Italy isn't part of Italy, and is the birthplace of one of the world's most influential figures. Corsica, located in the Tyrrhenian Sea, is perhaps the most beautiful European island in the world. Corsicans have their own language that is called Corse, and the island is where the mastermind Napoleon Bonaparte was born. If you haven't guessed it by now, this place is a part of France.
